title: Recruitment Module. In 'Ecological Field Monitoring Protocols Manual using the Ecological Monitoring System Australia'
author: McCallum, Kimberly
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This module is one of many that form the  Ecological Field Monitoring Protocols Manual  using the  Ecological Monitoring System Australia (EMSA),  available at  https://emsa.tern.org.au/documents . The Recruitment Module covers the procedures and guidelines of two protocols for recording age class structure and survivorship of vegetation within a core monitoring plot. Depending on the project aims and the system under study, recruitment surveys can consist of a single protocol or combination of the two protocols. The two protocols are: 
 
 Age class protocol – consists of two sub-protocols, the growth stage and life-stage surveys at the plot level (100 m x 100 m) and seedling, sapling and juvenile counts at the sub-plot level (40 x 40 m). 
 Survivorship protocol – enables users to tag individual plants with a unique ID, record location, health, growth stage and life-stage, and record size measurements for individual plants.
